about.text1: Computer science student at IUT of Calais for more than 2 years.
about.text2: I live in Calais and I am 20 years old. I am passionate about software development. I am also calm, rigorous and able to work as part of a team...

about.title: About
about.desc1: Passionate about computing from a young age, I specialized in application development.
about.desc2: A third-year Computer Science student at IUT de Calais, I have a growing interest in AI technologies.
about.desc3: I consider myself calm and thorough, someone who enjoys asking questions and seeking to understand.

bac.title: Baccalauréat
bac.description: Baccalauréat with specializations in Mathematics and Computer Science (NSI)

but1.title: BUT1
but1.description: First year of the Computer Science degree. I revisited programming and algorithmic fundamentals, discovered the design-before-code approach (entity-relationship and relational database modeling), and learned new languages such as Java, C, and PostgreSQL. I also participated for the first time in large group projects, including building a website and a text-based game in C.

but2.title: BUT2
but2.description: During this second year, I learned how to create, secure, document and consume a REST API. I also used frameworks such as Node.js, Vue.js, and Symfony to build web applications. Additionally, I learned how to write unit tests and set up CI/CD pipelines for automated deployment.

but3.title: BUT3
but3.description: In this third year, I studied the field of artificial intelligence, learning how to apply machine learning for regression and classification tasks. I also covered foundational concepts around the transformer model.

projects.title: My Projects
projects.intro: Throughout my studies, I had the opportunity to get involved in application development. I also had the chance to build websites, work on graphical interfaces and design databases...
projects.search: Search for a project by title, description or technology...

rando_passion.intro: The goal of this internship was to update the existing website, which served as a showcase for the association. This update comprised three main parts:
rando_passion.part1: The site needed to allow members to view upcoming hikes, browse photos from previous outings, and access an interactive map listing completed hikes. It also had to provide access to the current term's calendar, press articles, and registration information.
rando_passion.part2: This section needed to allow association members to add or modify hike-related data, while minimizing the steps required to ensure efficiency and ease of use. It also had to support member management, including assigning access rights, adding or removing members, and appointing or removing captains. In addition, it needed to handle articles and registration documents.
rando_passion.part3: The goal of this feature was to enable automatic email notifications to members in case of a hike cancellation. Additionally, managing hikes, articles, members, and captains required setting up a database capable of communicating with the website.

arcade.intro: This project aimed to perform maintenance on the IUT's arcade cabinet:
arcade.item4: Set up AI-powered scripts to automate certain tasks

traitement_image.intro: This project aimed to create software capable of retrieving satellite images from the MAST database and processing them to improve visibility and highlight specific features. I worked on two aspects: image transformation and image retrieval from the database.
traitement_image.part1: Development of a Python class to load, process and display astronomical images in FITS format, the standard in astrophysics.
traitement_image.part1Items: Loads scientific data from a space image
traitement_image.part1Items2: Cleans invalid values (NaN, infinities, negative values)
traitement_image.part1Items3: Automatically corrects image orientation from metadata
traitement_image.part1Items4: Applies gamma correction to improve contrast
traitement_image.part1Items6: Supports different colormaps (red, green, blue, grayscale)
traitement_image.part2: Development of a Python module to automatically search and download astronomical images in FITS format from the official MAST archive.
traitement_image.part2Items2: Searches public observations by specific criteria (mission, target)
traitement_image.part2Items3: Filters scientific products to keep only FITS files, calibrated data (level 2) and DRC images
traitement_image.part2Items4: Automatically selects one file per scientific filter
traitement_image.part2Items5: Downloads files from the official API
traitement_image.part2Items6: Tracks download progress in real time
traitement_image.part2End: The module is integrated into a graphical interface built with PyQt6, using a signal system to display the current file name, the start of a download, and the completion percentage.

vr_game.content: This project involved building a VR game in one week using Unreal Engine. The goal was to create a puzzle game requiring hand interactions. In this project, I worked on hand interactions and designed certain puzzles, including a bow and arrow mechanic used to lower a drawbridge.

xenon_space.intro: This project is a video game developed in Python. The goal was to create an AI that could play against human players or against another AI.
xenon_space.part1: In this project, I programmed the animation classes, the HUD creation, and the turn logic.
xenon_space.ai: The AI computes its next move based on the current game state. It takes into account several factors such as the position of ships (allied and enemy), ship health, and available actions (number of moves, possible attacks).

ihm.intro: A store inventory management application developed in a team of three for a university project, implementing MVC architecture and Dijkstra's algorithm.
ihm.presentation: This project involved creating an interface for a store to streamline inventory management. The goal was to apply a Model-View-Controller architecture and graph theory concepts from class, in particular Dijkstra's algorithm. We had to build two separate applications: one for store customers and one for internal store management.
ihm.role: I was responsible for developing the model layer of the project — the structure used to store and manipulate data throughout the application. I created numerous Python classes, including:
ihm.dijkstraDesc: Computes the shortest path through the store's grid from a starting point, enabling optimal routing of customers to products.
ihm.planDesc: Base class representing the store layout as a grid of points. Each point knows its neighbours, allowing Dijkstra's algorithm to traverse the graph.
ihm.produitDesc: Represents a store product with its properties (name, price, description, icon, type), used in both the customer and management applications.
